raw_consumer_statement: XX/XX/scrub>, 2025, Marcus by Goldman Sachs sent me a text if I would give permission to make a purchase at a store Called XXXX  located at XXXX XXXX. XXXX, XXXX XXXX, Ca. XXXX. I accidently text yes then I realized I did not want the purchase texted NO, TO LATE. So, I disputed the charge. I have disputed the charge now 8 times each time within 3 to 7 days they cancelled my dispute. I have talked to 12 different people in the company, 1 gentleman told me I had to prove my innocence myself so on XX/XX/year>2025, I called the XXXX XXXX talked to a security person and asked if he had pictures of a sale that was made on XX/XX/year>2025, between XXXX and XXXX that day. 4 hours later he said yes, the person that made the {$250.00} purchase of gift cards was a regular customer and somehow used my numbers to make the purchase. I sent all my information to Marcus. I have called XXXX and nobody from Marcus has ever called them to check on the identity of the purchaser. I have filed a police report with the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX case # XXXX.
